Title: Create a record with valid data
Steps: Open the page under test | Enter "Sample Username" in the Username field | Enter "emma.smith@example.com" in the Email field | Enter "9876543210" in the Phone field | Enter "34" in the Age field | Enter "Sample Notes" in the Notes field | Click the Register button
Expected:
✓ The User record is created successfully.
✓ A success confirmation message is displayed.
✓ The saved User shows the entered Username, Email, Phone, Age and Notes values exactly as entered.
✓ The new User appears in the User list.
✓ The new User is still shown in the User list after the page is refreshed.

Title: Duplicate / unique-constraint violation is handled
Steps: Open the page under test | Enter a Username that already exists (a duplicate of an existing record) in the Username field | Enter a Email that already exists (a duplicate of an existing record) in the Email field | Enter a Phone that already exists (a duplicate of an existing record) in the Phone field | Enter a Age that already exists (a duplicate of an existing record) in the Age field | Enter a Notes that already exists (a duplicate of an existing record) in the Notes field | Click the Register button
Expected:
✓ The duplicate is rejected — a second User record is NOT created.
✓ A clear "already exists" uniqueness error is shown.
✓ The original existing User is left unchanged.
✓ The total User count does not increase.

Title: Direct URL / API access is authorization-checked
Steps: Request the User Registration URL directly (bypassing the normal in-app navigation) | Use a session that is NOT authorized for this operation | Verify the direct request is rejected or redirected by a server-side authorization check (not merely hidden in the UI) | Verify the User resource is inaccessible and no User record is created
Expected:
✓ The operation is denied — no User is created or changed.
✓ The user sees an access-denied / not-authorised message (or is sent to the login page).
✓ The User list shows no new or changed User afterwards.

Title: Cancel discards input and returns without saving
Steps: Open the page under test | Enter "Sample Username" in the Username field | Enter "emma.smith@example.com" in the Email field | Enter "9876543210" in the Phone field | Enter "28" in the Age field | Enter "Sample Notes" in the Notes field | Click the Cancel button | Return to the list and confirm the record was NOT created (the entered data was discarded)
Expected:
✓ No User is created — the entered data is discarded.
✓ The user is returned to the User list without saving.
✓ The new User does not appear in the User list afterwards, confirming nothing was saved.
✓ Re-opening the form shows empty fields.

Title: Partial-name search returns the record
Steps: Open the page under test | Enter "Sample Username" in the Username field | Enter "emma.smith@example.com" in the Email field | Enter "9876543210" in the Phone field | Enter "51" in the Age field | Enter "Sample Notes" in the Notes field | Click the Register button | Open the records list / search page | Search for the newly created record (by its identifier and by name) | Confirm the newly created record appears in the search results
Expected:
✓ The newly created User is returned in the search results.
✓ It is found by its identifier and by name.
✓ The result appears immediately after creation, without needing to wait or search again.
✓ The returned record shows the exact values that were saved.

Title: Missing required fields are rejected
Steps: Open the page under test | Enter a blank Username (leave it empty) in the Username field | Enter a blank Email (leave it empty) in the Email field | Enter a blank Phone (leave it empty) in the Phone field | Enter a blank Age (leave it empty) in the Age field | Enter a blank Notes (leave it empty) in the Notes field | Click the Register button
Expected:
✓ The User is NOT created — no record is saved.
✓ A clear, specific validation error message is displayed.
✓ The form stays on screen with the entered values retained for correction.
✓ No new User appears in the User list.

Title: SQL-injection input is rejected safely
Steps: Open the page under test | Enter the SQL-injection string "' OR 1=1 --" in the Username field | Enter the SQL-injection string "' OR 1=1 --" in the Email field | Enter the SQL-injection string "' OR 1=1 --" in the Phone field | Enter the SQL-injection string "' OR 1=1 --" in the Age field | Enter the SQL-injection string "' OR 1=1 --" in the Notes field | Click the Register button
Expected:
✓ The input is rejected, or the User is created showing the text exactly as typed — the payload is treated as plain text, not run.
✓ No pop-up, alert box, or injected element appears on any User screen or list.
✓ Wherever the value is shown, it displays as the literal characters that were entered.
✓ A clear, generic error message is shown, with no internal or technical detail exposed to the user.

Title: XSS / script payload is escaped, not executed

Expected:
✓ The input is rejected, or the User is created showing the text exactly as typed — the payload is treated as plain text, not run.
✓ No pop-up, alert box, or injected element appears on any User screen or list.
✓ Wherever the value is shown, it displays as the literal characters that were entered.
✓ A clear, generic error message is shown, with no internal or technical detail exposed to the user.
